name: Tutorial 2: Part B GLOBE Candidate Analysis
description: Filters Medicare Part B drugs by spending threshold and IRA exclusion to identify GLOBE payment model candidates. Demonstrates spending analysis, reference table JOIN for IRA filtering, and high-spend candidate identification.

narration.pre: Starting a new analysis session. We will analyze Medicare Part B drug spending data to identify GLOBE payment model candidates — high-spend drugs that are not subject to IRA price negotiation.
narration.post: Session initialized. Part B data differs from Part D: drugs are identified by HCPCS code, and there is no manufacturer column. Let's import the dataset.

narration.pre: Importing the CMS Medicare Part B Drug Spending dataset. This is a 500-row sample containing HCPCS codes, brand names, generic names, and spending data across 5 years (2019-2023). Each row represents one drug identified by its HCPCS billing code.
narration.post: Dataset loaded: 500 rows, 47 columns. Part B data includes HCPCS_Cd (billing code), HCPCS_Desc (procedure description), brand and generic names, and per-year spending metrics. Note: unlike Part D, there is no manufacturer name column — drug identification is by HCPCS code + brand name.

narration.pre: Inspect the schema first. Part B has a different structure than Part D — it uses HCPCS billing codes instead of brand/generic name pairs, and has no manufacturer column.
narration.post: 47 columns. Key difference from Part D: HCPCS_Cd and HCPCS_Desc identify the drug instead of Brnd_Name alone. Spending columns follow the same Tot_Spndng / Avg_Spndng pattern. The Avg_DY23_ASP_Price column is the Average Sales Price — a Part B-specific pricing metric.

narration.pre: AI is analyzing the dataset structure to generate automatic insights.
narration.post: Insights generated. The AI identified spending concentration and year-over-year trends. Each card has Explore in Query to load the SQL. Now let's explore the spending distribution before applying GLOBE criteria.

details.sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT Brnd_Name) AS total_drugs FROM dataset

narration.pre: First, let's count how many distinct drugs are in the Part B dataset. Unlike Part D which had duplicate rows per manufacturer, Part B has one row per drug (HCPCS code).
narration.post: 500 distinct brand-name drugs in the dataset. This is our full universe of Part B drugs. Next we'll see which ones have the highest Medicare spending.

details.sql: SELECT Brnd_Name, Gnrc_Name, HCPCS_Cd, Tot_Spndng_2023, Tot_Benes_2023 FROM dataset ORDER BY Tot_Spndng_2023 DESC LIMIT 10

narration.pre: Let's see the top 10 Part B drugs by 2023 spending. Part B covers physician-administered drugs — primarily infusions and injections given in clinical settings.
narration.post: Top 10 spenders identified. Keytruda (pembrolizumab) leads with over $5B in Part B spending. Eylea, Prolia, and Darzalex Faspro follow. These are primarily oncology and specialty drugs administered in hospitals and clinics.

narration.pre: Loading the IRA Negotiated Drugs reference table. We'll use this to identify which Part B drugs are already subject to IRA price negotiation — these must be excluded from GLOBE candidate lists.
narration.post: Reference table loaded: 35 IRA drugs. Note that most IRA-negotiated drugs are Part D (oral/retail) drugs. Only a handful appear in Part B (physician-administered). The JOIN will tell us exactly how many.

details.sql: SELECT DISTINCT d.Brnd_Name, d.Gnrc_Name, d.HCPCS_Cd, r.ira_round FROM dataset d INNER JOIN reference r ON d.Brnd_Name = r.drug_name ORDER BY r.ira_round, d.Brnd_Name

narration.pre: Now let's see which Part B drugs match the IRA negotiated list. We expect fewer matches than Part D since most IRA drugs are oral medications dispensed at pharmacies, not physician-administered injections.
narration.post: Only 6 Part B drugs match the IRA list. This is expected — the IRA primarily targets high-cost retail drugs. These 6 will be excluded from our GLOBE candidate list.

details.sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT d.Brnd_Name) AS high_spend_non_ira FROM dataset d LEFT JOIN reference r ON d.Brnd_Name = r.drug_name WHERE r.drug_name IS NULL AND d.Tot_Spndng_2023 > 100000000

narration.pre: Now the key GLOBE filter: how many non-IRA Part B drugs have spending above $100 million? The GLOBE model targets high-spend single-source drugs, so a spending threshold is the first filter.
narration.post: 89 non-IRA Part B drugs exceed $100M in 2023 spending. These are the initial GLOBE candidate pool. The next step would be to apply single-source criteria and therapeutic category classification, but this spending filter alone narrows the field significantly.

details.sql: SELECT d.Brnd_Name, d.Gnrc_Name, d.HCPCS_Cd, d.Tot_Spndng_2023, d.Tot_Benes_2023, d.Avg_Spndng_Per_Bene_2023 FROM dataset d LEFT JOIN reference r ON d.Brnd_Name = r.drug_name WHERE r.drug_name IS NULL AND d.Tot_Spndng_2023 > 100000000 ORDER BY d.Tot_Spndng_2023 DESC LIMIT 20

narration.pre: Finally, let's see the top 20 GLOBE candidates ranked by spending. These are the highest-value non-IRA Part B drugs — the most likely targets for the GLOBE payment model.
narration.post: Top 20 GLOBE candidates identified. These represent the highest-spend physician-administered drugs not subject to IRA negotiation. In a full analysis, the next steps would be: apply single-source criteria (no biosimilar competition), verify orphan drug exclusions, and classify by USP therapeutic category.

narration.pre: Exporting the top 20 GLOBE candidates to Excel for further analysis.
narration.post: Export complete. The Excel file contains the ranked candidate list ready for policy review and additional filtering (orphan drug status, biosimilar availability, USP category classification).

Session summary: Started with 500 Part B drugs, identified 6 IRA-negotiated drugs via reference table JOIN, found 89 non-IRA drugs exceeding $100M in spending, and exported the top 20 candidates. Part B analysis is simpler than Part D (no manufacturer filtering needed) but the workflow pattern is identical: import, reference JOIN, threshold filter, export.

resume_state.last_sql: SELECT d.Brnd_Name, d.Gnrc_Name, d.HCPCS_Cd, d.Tot_Spndng_2023, d.Tot_Benes_2023, d.Avg_Spndng_Per_Bene_2023 FROM dataset d LEFT JOIN reference r ON d.Brnd_Name = r.drug_name WHERE r.drug_name IS NULL AND d.Tot_Spndng_2023 > 100000000 ORDER BY d.Tot_Spndng_2023 DESC LIMIT 20
